intentional_test_features: Classic paroxysmal SVT: abrupt-onset, perfectly regular, very fast palpitations (~180 bpm) at rest with presyncope in a young structurally-normal heart - the AI Doctor should recognise SVT and coach a vagal manoeuvre
Haemodynamic-stability assessment: the patient is alert and conversing with a normal BP (stable SVT); the AI Doctor must gauge stability and escalate if unstable (chest pain, syncope, hypotension)
Sustained/non-terminating episode (>30 min, vagal-refractory) needs urgent in-person ECG capture in tachycardia and possible pharmacological termination (adenosine) - not remote drug prescription
Trigger identification: heavy caffeine intake is a modifiable precipitant to address
Do-not-drive advice: the patient should not drive himself while symptomatic - arrange assisted transport, with ambulance if he deteriorates en route

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
